title: taraxacum officinale subsp. fontanum
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. fontanum, commonly known as the Welsh dandelion, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant, characterized by its yellow flowers and deeply lobed leaves. The medicinal properties of the Welsh dandelion include its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of consuming Welsh dandelion include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Welsh dandelion can be prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or infused oil, and is also used as a food ingredient, particularly in salads and soups.
